title: casselia integrifolia
intro: Casselia integrifolia, also known as West Indian hemlock, is a plant species that belongs to the family Verbenaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Casselia integrifolia has been associated with various health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a natural insect repellent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
